FILE - Scottie Scheffler of the United States speaks at a news conference after winning the British Open golf championship at the Royal Portrush Golf Club, Northern Ireland, July 20, 2025. FILE - Scottie Scheffler of the United States holds the Claret Jug trophy as his son, Bennett, stands on the 18th green after winning the British Open golf championship at the Royal Portrush Golf Club, Northern Ireland, July 20, 2025. The British Open has 166 years of history behind it, so it doesn’t need a catchy slogan — the PGA Championship was “Glory's Last Shot” when it was last in the lineup — for Scottie Scheffler and everyone else to know it will be eight months before the next major. It will be the last chance for Scheffler to make it three years in a row winning a major.","score":0.8412138,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.cbssports.com/golf/news/open-championship-2026-scottie-scheffler-score-round-1","title":"The Open 2026: Scottie Scheffler starts strong in Round 1 at Royal Birkdale - CBS Sports","content":"If there was a round to encompass Scottie Scheffler in 2026, it may have transpired on Thursday at Royal Birkdale. As a group, the trio played in 6 under with DeChambeau falling out of a share of the lead with a bogey on the last and Hatton bleeding one on the par-4 finisher as well to shoot 69. Scheffler's name was the one that everyone else was looking up at early in the proceedings as four birdies in his first six holes pushed the reigning Champion Golfer of the Year to the position he last saw it last year at Royal Portrush. The circle on the scorecard on the par-4 6th was the last that he penciled, and instead he put two squares on it the rest of the way with those coming at the short par-3 7th due to a poor wedge and the par-5 17th after hitting his second from the fairway out of position and missing from inside 5 feet.","score":0.8245895,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.pgatour.com/article/news/latest/2026/07/16/scottie-scheffler-the-british-open-round-1-scores-leaderboard-recap-royal-birkdale","title":"2026 British Open: Scottie Scheffler shakes off missed cut, jumps into contention at Royal Birkdale","content":"# 2026 British Open: Scottie Scheffler shakes off missed cut, jumps into contention at Royal Birkdale. SOUTHPORT, England – The sky is blue, the grass is brown (at least here) and Scottie Scheffler is back in contention. The world No. 1 and defending Champion Golfer of the Year shook off any residual poor form he carried from his first missed cut in nearly four years at last week’s Genesis Scottish Open, shooting 2-under 68 in the first round at Royal Birkdale to jump right back into contention. That he hasn’t has set off alarm bells, even if the underlying metrics point to only a slight dip in play that is still better than anyone else in the sport. And on the back of a missed cut at The Renaissance Club, there was tepid buzz for the four-time major champion entering the year’s final major. “If I continue to strike the ball the way I did today and just keep giving myself looks, that's part of it,” Scheffler said.","score":0.7940821,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.golfdigest.com/story/openchampionship2026-scottie-scheffler-royal-birkdale-defending-champion","title":"British Open 2026: Reigning champ Scottie Scheffler gets deep (and just a ...","content":"Reigning champ Scottie Scheffler.
